records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2669 - Imam (as) said: "There is no harm if a Muhrim washes khaluq (a fragrant substance) off his garment."
records.arabicText:
2669 - وَ قَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَغْسِلَ اَلرَّجُلُ اَلْخَلُوقَ عَنْ ثَوْبِهِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ».
وإذا اضطر المحرم إلى سعوط فيه مسك من ريح يعرض له في وجهه وعلة تصيبه فلا بأس بأن يستعط به
فَقَدْ سَأَلَ إِسْمَاعِيلُ بْنُ جَابِرٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ ذَلِكَ فَقَالَ «اِسْتَعِطْ بِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2669 - Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a Muhrim washes khaluq (a fragrant substance) off his garment."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2670 - Al-Halabi and Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"A Muhrim may cover his nose from a pleasant fragrance but should not cover his nose from an unpleasant odor."
records.arabicText: 2670 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ وَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْمُحْرِمُ يُمْسِكُ عَلَى أَنْفِهِ مِنَ اَلرِّيحِ اَلطَّيِّبَةِ وَ لاَ يُمْسِكُ عَلَى أَنْفِهِ مِنَ اَلرِّيحِ اَلْخَبِيثَةِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2670 - Al-Halabi and Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A Muhrim may cover his nose from a pleasant fragrance but should not cover his nose from an unpleasant odor."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2671 - Hisham ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm in the pleasant fragrances encountered between Safa and Marwah from the perfumers' shops, and he should not cover his nose."
records.arabicText: 2671 - وَ رَوَى هِشَامُ بْنُ اَلْحَكَمِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالرِّيحِ اَلطَّيِّبَةِ فِيمَا بَيْنَ اَلصَّفَا وَ اَلْمَرْوَةِ مِنْ رِيحِ اَلْعَطَّارِينَ وَ لاَ يُمْسِكُ عَلَى أَنْفِهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2671 - Hisham ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in the pleasant fragrances encountered between Safa and Marwah from the perfumers' shops, and he should not cover his nose."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2672 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm in smelling idhkhar (a type of fragrant grass), qaysum (a medicinal herb), khuzama (lavender), shiḥ (wormwood), and similar plants while you are in Ihram."
Ali ibn Mahziyar reported:
I asked Ibn Abi Umayr about apples, citrons (utruj), lotus fruit (nabq), and other fragrant items. Imam (as) said: "Refrain from smelling or eating them." However, he did not narrate anything specific regarding their permissibility.
records.arabicText:
2672 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تَشَمَّ اَلْإِذْخِرَ وَ اَلْقَيْصُومَ وَ اَلْخُزَامَى وَ اَلشِّيحَ وَ أَشْبَاهَهُ وَ أَنْتَ مُحْرِمٌ».
وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَهْزِيَارَ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُ اِبْنَ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنِ اَلتُّفَّاحِ وَ اَلْأُتْرُجِّ وَ اَلنَّبِقِ وَ مَا طَابَ مِنْ رِيحِهِ فَقَالَ تُمْسِكُ عَنْ شَمِّهِ وَ أَكْلِهِ وَ لَمْ يَرْوِ فِيهِ شَيْئاً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2672 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no harm in smelling idhkhar (a type of fragrant grass), qaysum (a medicinal herb), khuzama (lavender), shiḥ (wormwood), and similar plants while you are in Ihram."
Ali ibn Mahziyar reported:
I asked Ibn Abi Umayr about apples, citrons (utruj), lotus fruit (nabq), and other fragrant items. Imam (as) said: "Refrain from smelling or eating them." However, he did not narrate anything specific regarding their permissibility.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2673 - It was narrated from Abdullah ibn al-Mughira, who said:
I said to Abu al-Hasan the First (as): "Can I use a shade while I am in Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "No."
I asked: "What if I use a shade and offer expiation?"
Imam (as) said: "No."
I asked: "What if I fall ill?"
Imam (as) said: "Use a shade and offer expiation."
Then Imam (as) said: "Do you not know that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said: 'There is no pilgrim who exposes himself to the sun while reciting the Talbiyah until the sun sets except that his sins disappear with the setting of the sun.'"
records.arabicText: 2673 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ قَالَ : قُلْتُ لِأَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلْأَوَّلِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أُظَلِّلُ وَ أَنَا مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ «لاَ» قُلْتُ فَأُظَلِّلُ وَ أُكَفِّرُ قَالَ «لاَ» قُلْتُ فَإِنْ مَرِضْتُ قَالَ «ظَلِّلْ وَ كَفِّرْ » ثُمَّ قَالَ «أَ مَا عَلِمْتَ أَنَّ رَسُولَ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ قَالَ «مَا مِنْ حَاجٍّ يَضْحَى مُلَبِّياً حَتَّى تَغِيبَ اَلشَّمْسُ إِلاَّ غَابَتْ ذُنُوبُهُ مَعَهَا ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2673 - It was narrated from Abdullah ibn al-Mughira, who said:
I said to Abu al-Hasan the First (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"Can I use a shade while I am in Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "No."
I asked: "What if I use a shade and offer expiation?"
Imam (as) said: "No."
I asked: "What if I fall ill?"
Imam (as) said: "Use a shade and offer expiation."
Then Imam (as) said: "Do you not know that the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said: 'There is no pilgrim who exposes himself to the sun while reciting the Talbiyah until the sun sets except that his sins disappear with the setting of the sun.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2674 - It was narrated from Al-Husayn ibn Muslim, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far the Second (as): "What is the difference between a tent (fusṭāṭ) and the shade of a litter (maḥmil)?"
Imam (as) said: "One should not seek shade in the maḥmil. The difference between them is like the case of a woman who menstruates during Month of Ramadan; she makes up the fasts but does not make up the prayers."
I replied: "You have spoken the truth, may I be your ransom."
records.arabicText: 2674 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنِ اَلْحُسَيْنِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ مَا فَرْقُ مَا بَيْنَ اَلْفُسْطَاطِ وَ بَيْنَ ظِلِّ اَلْمَحْمِلِ قَالَ «لاَ يَنْبَغِي أَنْ يُسْتَظَلَّ فِي اَلْمَحْمِلِ وَ اَلْفَرْقُ بَيْنَهُمَا أَنَّ اَلْمَرْأَةَ تَطْمَثُ فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ فَتَقْضِي اَلصِّيَامَ وَ لاَ تَقْضِي اَلصَّلاَةَ» قَالَ صَدَقْتَ جُعِلْتُ فِدَاكَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2674 - It was narrated from Al-Husayn ibn Muslim, who said:
I asked Abu Ja'far the Second (as): "What is the difference between a tent (fusṭāṭ) and the shade of a litter (maḥmil)?"
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"One should not seek shade in the maḥmil. The difference between them is like the case of a woman who menstruates during Month of Ramadan; she makes up the fasts but does not make up the prayers."
I replied: "You have spoken the truth, may I be your ransom."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2675 - Ali ibn Mahziyar narrated from Bakr ibn Salih, who said:
I wrote to Abu Ja'far the Second (as), saying: "My aunt is with me, and she is my travel companion. She suffers greatly from the heat when she is in Ihram. Do you permit me to use shade for both of us?"
Imam (as) wrote back: "Shade her alone."
records.arabicText: 2675 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ مَهْزِيَارَ عَنْ بَكْرِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ قَالَ كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ اَلثَّانِي عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّ عَمَّتِي مَعِي وَ هِيَ زَمِيلَتِي وَ يَشْتَدُّ عَلَيْهَا اَلْحَرُّ إِذَا أَحْرَمَتْ فَتَرَى أَنْ أُظَلِّلَ عَلَيَّ وَ عَلَيْهَا فَكَتَبَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «ظَلِّلْ عَلَيْهَا وَحْدَهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2675 - Ali ibn Mahziyar narrated from Bakr ibn Salih, who said:
I wrote to Abu Ja'far the Second (as), saying:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"My aunt is with me, and she is my travel companion. She suffers greatly from the heat when she is in Ihram. Do you permit me to use shade for both of us?"
Imam (as) wrote back: "Shade her alone."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2676 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Basir, who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a woman placing a shade over herself while she is in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "What about a man placing a shade over himself while in Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he is afflicted with severe pain (shaqiqah), and he should give charity of a mudd for each day."
records.arabicText: 2676 - وَ رَوَى اَلْبَزَنْطِيُّ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ أَبِي حَمْزَةَ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَضْرِبُ عَلَيْهَا اَلظِّلاَلَ وَ هِيَ مُحْرِمَةٌ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ» قُلْتُ فَالرَّجُلُ يَضْرِبُ عَلَيْهِ اَلظِّلاَلَ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ «نَعَمْ إِذَا كَانَتْ بِهِ شَقِيقَةٌ وَ يَتَصَدَّقُ بِمُدٍّ لِكُلِّ يَوْمٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2676 - Al-Bazanti narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from Abu Basir, who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a woman placing a shade over herself while she is in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
I asked: "What about a man placing a shade over himself while in Ihram?"
Imam (as) said: "Yes, if he is afflicted with severe pain (shaqiqah), and he should give charity of a mudd for each day."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2677 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' narrated that Abu al-Hasan (as) was asked—while I was listening—about a person in the state of ihram seeking shade due to harm from rain or the sun, or, he said, due to illness.
Imam (as) commanded that a sheep should be sacrificed in Mina and said: "When we want to do this, we seek shade and offer the expiation."
records.arabicText: 2677 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ : أَنَّهُ سُئِلَ أَبُو اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا أَسْمَعُ عَنِ اَلظِّلِّ لِلْمُحْرِمِ فِي أَذًى مِنْ مَطَرٍ أَوْ شَمْسٍ أَوْ قَالَ مِنْ عِلَّةٍ فَأَمَرَ بِفِدَاءِ شَاةٍ يَذْبَحُهَا بِمِنًى وَ قَالَ «نَحْنُ إِذَا أَرَدْنَا ذَلِكَ ظَلَّلْنَا وَ فَدَيْنَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2677 - Muhammad ibn Isma'il ibn Bazi' narrated that Abu al-Hasan (as) was asked—while I was listening—about a person in the state of ihram seeking shade due to harm from rain or the sun, or, he said, due to illness.
Imam (as) commanded that a sheep should be sacrificed in Mina and said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When we want to do this, we seek shade and offer the expiation."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2678 - In the narration of Hariz, Abu Abdullah (as) said: "There is no harm in a tent being set up over women and children while they are in the state of ihram. However, the person in ihram must not immerse themselves in water, nor should someone who is fasting."
records.arabicText: 2678 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَرِيزٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِالْقُبَّةِ عَلَى اَلنِّسَاءِ وَ اَلصِّبْيَانِ وَ هُمْ مُحْرِمُونَ وَ لاَ يَرْتَمِسُ اَلْمُحْرِمُ فِي اَلْمَاءِ وَ لاَ اَلصَّائِمُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2678 - In the narration of Hariz, Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in a tent being set up over women and children while they are in the state of ihram. However, the person in ihram must not immerse themselves in water, nor should someone who is fasting."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2679 - It is narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim who said: "I saw Abu Abdullah (as) perform ablution while he was in the state of ihram, and then he took a towel and wiped his face with it."
records.arabicText: 2679 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ مَنْصُورِ بْنِ حَازِمٍ قَالَ : رَأَيْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ قَدْ تَوَضَّأَ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ ثُمَّ أَخَذَ مِنْدِيلاً فَمَسَحَ بِهِ وَجْهَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2679 - It is narrated from Mansur ibn Hazim who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I saw Abu Abdullah (as) perform ablution while he was in the state of ihram, and then he took a towel and wiped his face with it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2680 - It is narrated by Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "It is disliked for the person in ihram to cover their nose with their garment. However, there is no harm if the person in ihram extends their garment until it reaches their nose."
records.arabicText: 2680 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «يُكْرَهُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَنْ يَجُوزَ بِثَوْبِهِ فَوْقَ أَنْفِهِ وَ لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ يَمُدَّ اَلْمُحْرِمُ ثَوْبَهُ حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ أَنْفَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2680 - It is narrated by Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "It is disliked for the person in ihram to cover their nose with their garment. However, there is no harm if the person in ihram extends their garment until it reaches their nose."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2681 - Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari and Hisham ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said: "It is disliked for the person in ihram to let their garment cover their nose from below."
Imam (as) also said, "Show openness to the One for whom you have entered the state of ihram."
records.arabicText: 2681 - أَنَّ حَفْصَ بْنَ اَلْبَخْتَرِيِّ وَ هِشَامَ بْنَ اَلْحَكَمِ رَوَيَا عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «يُكْرَهُ لِلْمُحْرِمِ أَنْ يَجُوزَ ثَوْبُهُ أَنْفَهُ مِنْ أَسْفَلَ» وَ قَالَ «اِضْحَ لِمَنْ أَحْرَمْتَ لَهُ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2681 - Hafs ibn al-Bakhtari and Hisham ibn al-Hakam narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"It is disliked for the person in ihram to let their garment cover their nose from below."
Imam (as) also said, "Show openness to the One for whom you have entered the state of ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2682 - It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan who said: "I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say to my father, who complained to him about the heat of the sun while he was in the state of ihram and was troubled by it.
He asked, 'Do you think it is permissible for me to cover myself with the edge of my garment?'
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm in that as long as it does not touch your head.'"
records.arabicText: 2682 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ : سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يَقُولُ لِأَبِي وَ شَكَا إِلَيْهِ حَرَّ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ وَ هُوَ يَتَأَذَّى بِهِ وَ قَالَ تَرَى أَنْ أَسْتَتِرَ بِطَرَفِ ثَوْبِي قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ مَا لَمْ يُصِبْ رَأْسَكَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2682 - It is narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"I heard Abu Abdullah (as) say to my father, who complained to him about the heat of the sun while he was in the state of ihram and was troubled by it.
He asked, 'Do you think it is permissible for me to cover myself with the edge of my garment?'
Imam (as) said: 'There is no harm in that as long as it does not touch your head.'"

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2683 - Saeed al-A'raj asked him about a person in the state of ihram seeking shade from the sun using a stick or their hand.
Imam (as) said: "No, except in the case of an illness."
records.arabicText: 2683 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَعِيدٌ اَلْأَعْرَجُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَسْتَتِرُ مِنَ اَلشَّمْسِ بِعُودِ أَوْ بِيَدِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ إِلاَّ مِنْ عِلَّةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2683 - Saeed al-A'raj asked him about a person in the state of ihram seeking shade from the sun using a stick or their hand.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "No, except in the case of an illness."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2684 - Al-Halabi asked him about a person in the state of ihram who covers their head unintentionally or while sleeping.
Imam (as) said: "They should recite the Talbiyah when they remember."
records.arabicText: 2684 - وَ سَأَلَهُ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يُغَطِّي رَأْسَهُ نَاسِياً أَوْ نَائِماً فَقَالَ «يُلَبِّي إِذَا ذَكَرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2684 - Al-Halabi asked him about a person in the state of ihram who covers their head unintentionally or while sleeping.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "They should recite the Talbiyah when they remember."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2685 - In the narration of Hariz: "They should remove the covering, recite the Talbiyah, and there is nothing upon them (no penalty).
records.arabicText: 2685 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَرِيزٍ : «يُلْقِي اَلْقِنَاعَ وَ يُلَبِّي وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ شَيْءٌ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2685 - In the narration of Hariz:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "They should remove the covering, recite the Talbiyah, and there is nothing upon them (no penalty).

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2686 - He was asked about a person in the state of ihram sleeping on their face while on their mount.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."
records.arabicText: 2686 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَنَامُ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ وَ هُوَ عَلَى رَاحِلَتِهِ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِذَلِكَ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2686 - He was asked about a person in the state of ihram sleeping on their face while on their mount.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in that."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2687 - Zurara asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a person in the state of ihram who has flies landing on their face, preventing them from sleeping, and whether they may cover their face when they wish to sleep.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 2687 - وَ سَأَلَ زُرَارَةُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَقَعُ اَلذُّبَابُ عَلَى وَجْهِهِ حِينَ يُرِيدُ اَلنَّوْمَ فَيَمْنَعُهُ مِنَ اَلنَّوْمِ أَ يُغَطِّي وَجْهَهُ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَنَامَ قَالَ «نَعَمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2687 - Zurara asked Abu Ja'far (as) about a person in the state of ihram who has flies landing on their face, preventing them from sleeping, and whether they may cover their face when they wish to sleep.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "Yes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2688 - Zurara narrated from Abu Abdullah (as): "A woman in the state of ihram should let her garment hang down to her chest."
records.arabicText: 2688 - وَ رَوَى زُرَارَةُ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ اَلْمُحْرِمَةَ تَسْدُلُ ثَوْبَهَا إِلَى نَحْرِهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2688 - Zurara narrated from Abu Abdullah (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman in the state of ihram should let her garment hang down to her chest."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2689 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man in the state of ihram who trimmed one of his nails.
Imam (as) said: "He must give a mudd of food as expiation. If he trims ten nails, he must sacrifice a sheep."
I asked: "What if he trims the nails of both his hands and feet?"
Imam (as) said: "If he does this in one sitting, he must offer one sacrifice. If he does it in two separate sittings, he must offer two sacrifices."
records.arabicText: 2689 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ قَلَّمَ ظُفُراً مِنْ أَظَافِيرِهِ وَ هُوَ مُحْرِمٌ قَالَ «عَلَيْهِ مُدٌّ مِنْ طَعَامٍ حَتَّى يَبْلُغَ عَشَرَةً فَإِنْ قَلَّمَ أَصَابِعَ يَدَيْهِ كُلَّهَا فَعَلَيْهِ دَمُ شَاةٍ» قُلْتُ فَإِنْ قَلَّمَ أَظَافِيرَ يَدَيْهِ وَ رِجْلَيْهِ جَمِيعاً فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ فِي مَجْلِسٍ وَاحِدٍ فَعَلَيْهِ دَمٌ وَ إِنْ كَانَ فَعَلَهُ مُتَفَرِّقاً فِي مَجْلِسَيْنِ فَعَلَيْهِ دَمَانِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2689 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab, from Abu Basir, who said: I asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a man in the state of ihram who trimmed one of his nails.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "He must give a mudd of food as expiation. If he trims ten nails, he must sacrifice a sheep."
I asked: "What if he trims the nails of both his hands and feet?"
Imam (as) said: "If he does this in one sitting, he must offer one sacrifice. If he does it in two separate sittings, he must offer two sacrifices."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2690 - In the narration of Zurara from Abu Ja'far (as): "Whoever does that (trims nails) out of forgetfulness, inattentiveness, or ignorance, there is nothing upon them (no penalty)."
records.arabicText: 2690 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «أَنَّ مَنْ فَعَلَ ذَلِكَ نَاسِياً أَوْ سَاهِياً أَوْ جَاهِلاً فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2690 - In the narration of Zurara from Abu Ja'far (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Whoever does that (trims nails) out of forgetfulness, inattentiveness, or ignorance, there is nothing upon them (no penalty)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2691 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a person in the state of ihram whose nails grow long or some of them break and cause harm.
Imam (as) said: "They should not cut any of them if they can avoid it. But if they cause harm, they may trim them, and for each nail, they must give a handful of food as expiation."
records.arabicText: 2691 - وَ سَأَلَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ تَطُولُ أَظْفَارَهُ أَوْ يَنْكَسِرُ بَعْضُهَا فَيُؤْذِيهِ ذَلِكَ قَالَ «لاَ يَقُصَّ مِنْهَا شَيْئاً إِنِ اِسْتَطَاعَ فَإِنْ كَانَتْ تُؤْذِيهِ فَلْيَقُصَّهَا وَ لْيُطْعِمْ مَكَانَ كُلِّ ظُفُرٍ قَبْضَةً مِنْ طَعَامٍ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2691 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar asked Abu Abdullah (as) about a person in the state of ihram whose nails grow long or some of them break and cause harm.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "They should not cut any of them if they can avoid it. But if they cause harm, they may trim them, and for each nail, they must give a handful of food as expiation."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2692 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim (as) about a man who forgot to trim his nails before entering the state of ihram until he had already entered it.
Imam (as) said: "He should leave them as they are."
I said: "One of our companions advised him to trim his nails and renew his ihram, so he did."
Imam (as) said: "He must offer a sacrifice (as expiation)."
records.arabicText: 2692 - وَ سَأَلَ إِسْحَاقُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ أَبَا إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ رَجُلٍ نَسِيَ أَنْ يُقَلِّمَ أَظَافِيرَهُ عِنْدَ اَلْإِحْرَامِ حَتَّى أَحْرَمَ قَالَ «يَدَعُهَا» قُلْتُ فَإِنَّ رَجُلاً مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا أَفْتَاهُ أَنْ يُقَلِّمَ أَظَافِيرَهُ وَ يُعِيدَ إِحْرَامَهُ فَفَعَلَ فَقَالَ «عَلَيْهِ دَمٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2692 - Ishaq ibn Ammar asked Abu Ibrahim (as) about a man who forgot to trim his nails before entering the state of ihram until he had already entered it.
records.thaqalaynMatn:
Imam (as) said: "He should leave them as they are."
I said: "One of our companions advised him to trim his nails and renew his ihram, so he did."
Imam (as) said: "He must offer a sacrifice (as expiation)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2693 - Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "If a man plucks his underarm hair after entering the state of ihram, he must offer a sacrifice (as expiation)."
records.arabicText: 2693 - وَ رَوَى حَرِيزٌ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «إِذَا نَتَفَ اَلرَّجُلُ إِبْطَهُ بَعْدَ اَلْإِحْرَامِ فَعَلَيْهِ دَمٌ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2693 - Hariz narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a man plucks his underarm hair after entering the state of ihram, he must offer a sacrifice (as expiation)."
